mnemonics.title: Dual-Mode Values
mnemonics.content: **0 for K**ernel, **1 for U**ser. Think: 'The OS is number zero (boss); the user is number one (citizen).' Or: Kernel is closer to hardware (0 = ground level).

mnemonics.title: Privileged Instructions
mnemonics.content: **TIMID** - Timer management, I/O operations, Memory map changes, Interrupt control, Device management. These can only run in kernel mode.

mnemonics.title: Trap vs Interrupt
mnemonics.content: **T**rap = **T**riggered by software (synchronous), **I**nterrupt = **I**nitiated by hardware (asynchronous). Traps are predictable; interrupts can happen anytime.

mnemonics.title: Timer Protection Chain
mnemonics.content: **SET-DEC-TRAP**: OS SETs the counter, clock DECrements it each tick, when zero a TRAP (interrupt) transfers control back to OS. This prevents CPU monopolization.

mnemonics.title: System Call Flow
mnemonics.content: **UTES-R**: User Traps, Enter kernel, Service request, Return to user. The five-step flow when a system call occurs.
